role: A React toggle component that provides a time-range slider for selecting a historical snapshot or returning to the live present.
capabilities: Renders a slider mapped between corpusStart and now
Computes fractional position from an ISO at timestamp
Sets at to an ISO timestamp on slider change
Snaps at to null (live present) when slider is near the far right
Displays a formatted date label or 'now · live'
Provides a button to reset to the live present
substance_why: The component implements complete slider logic, date parsing, formatting, null/live snapping, and a reset button with wired handlers.
interactions_present: Range slider onChange updates the selected time
Button click resets at to null (live present)
interactions_missing: No keyboard step or PageUp/PageDown handling beyond native range
No debouncing or throttling of slider input
No visible start/end tick labels or tooltips while dragging
No announcement of time change for screen readers
No handling for invalid or non-ISO date strings beyond silent null return
performance_risks: Date.parse(corpusStart) and Date.parse(now) run on every render without memoization
onChange creates a new closure and Date object on every slider event
fmt() constructs a new Date and locale string on every render
reuse_verdict_why: The time-scrubber concept and behavior are sound, but the date math, formatting, and slider handling should be extracted and memoized for the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Extract date-range math and fraction conversion into a reusable hook or utility
Extract date formatting into a shared formatter
Separate the slider thumb/label UI from the time-state logic
